seo.title: Flowise Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Flowise agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Flowise to build AI-driven chatflows for customer support, product recommendations, and pricing queries face heightened compliance risks due to dynamic LLM outputs that can misrepresent products, pricing, or policies. Without guardrails, unvalidated intermediate outputs or poor context handoffs in multi-agent flows can lead to unsubstantiated claims, inaccurate pricing, or non-compliant data handling, triggering FTC scrutiny, CCPA violations, or PCI DSS breaches. Vex provides runtime compliance checks to ensure Flowise chatflows deliver accurate, regulated responses while maintaining seamless user experiences.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims in AI-generated responses must be substantiated with evidence; unsubstantiated superiority claims or false endorsements are prohibited.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates product claims against a knowledge base of verified specs using semantic similarity checks in chatflow callbacks, blocking unsubstantiated outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data in chatflows must not be processed without consent; PII must be redacted from vector stores and agent memory.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex's PII detection guardrail scans intermediate outputs and vector retrievals, enforcing data minimization before agent handoffs.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment-related flows must not expose card details; pricing tools must accurately quote without manipulation.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex monitors custom tool nodes for PCI violations, validating pricing accuracy against canonical sources in sequential flows.

complianceRequirements.description: AI responses must be accessible, avoiding jargon or complex language that excludes users with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex applies readability and inclusivity checks to final outputs, ensuring WCAG-compliant language in customer-facing chatflows.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: Chatflow chains generate promotional claims without evidence, violating FTC guidelines.
industryRisks.example: AI recommends 'best organic shirt' without sales data, leading to FTC complaint.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Pricing Quotes
industryRisks.description: Vector retrieval pulls outdated prices, causing refund disputes and revenue loss.
industryRisks.example: $29.99 shirt quoted as $24.99 due to stale RAG documents.

industryRisks.name: PII Leakage in Agent Handoffs
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent flows pass customer emails between nodes without redaction, breaching CCPA.
industryRisks.example: Support agent shares customer's full address with refund agent unredacted.

industryRisks.name: Silent Tool Failures
industryRisks.description: Custom payment lookup tools fail without propagation, giving false availability.
industryRisks.example: Inventory tool crashes, AI promises out-of-stock item delivery.

implementation.title: Add Vex Callback to Flowise Chatflow
implementation.description: Integrate Vex as a callback handler in Flowise configuration to validate all intermediate chain outputs against e-commerce policies.

implementation.title: Guard Vector Store Retrieval
implementation.description: Ensure RAG retrievals return pricing/policy docs with >0.8 relevance, blocking low-quality responses.

implementation.title: Monitor Multi-Agent Context Handoffs
implementation.description: Validate context preservation and PII redaction between sequential agents handling returns/refunds.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Flowise in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Fashion retailer TrendThread deployed a Flowise chatflow for their e-commerce site, combining RAG for product catalog search, a pricing tool node for dynamic quotes, and multi-agent handoff from recommendation agent to support agent for returns processing. The flow handled 10K daily queries, pulling from a vector store of 50K product docs and inventory APIs. However, without validation, the chain produced unsubstantiated claims like 'our jeans are 30% more durable than competitors' from hallucinated intermediate outputs, vector retrieval occasionally returned irrelevant accessories for clothing queries (relevance <0.6), and the refund agent received unredacted customer CC numbers from the pricing tool due to silent failures in PII filtering.

This led to mounting customer complaints, a class-action CCPA notice, and FTC inquiry after viral social media posts about false advertising. Pricing errors caused $50K in refund disputes when AI quoted outdated sale prices.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex callbacks across chain ends, vector retrievals, and agent handoffs, TrendThread achieved 99.7% compliance rate. FTC claims were auto-blocked pre-response, pricing accuracy hit 100% via canonical validation, and PII leakage dropped to zero with redaction enforcement. Customer satisfaction rose 28%, refund disputes fell 92%, and the FTC inquiry was closed with no action—saving $2.3M in potential fines while scaling to 50K daily queries.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
